rows.design_decision: Applying 2D-DFT angular frequency analysis on the 9x9 angular sampling matrix (MRI k-space analogy) for material classification
rows.motivation: Traditional light field depth estimation relies on pure Lambertian assumptions and fails to distinguish specular, scattering, and diffuse reflections, causing depth estimation failure in non-Lambertian regions.
rows.sota_gap: Compared to heuristic highlight detection or complex CNN material segmentation, 2D-DFT parses physical wave vector changes directly in the frequency domain, reducing computational complexity to O(HW).
rows.scenario: Highly reflective and complex scattering material regions in the Non-lambertian_zhenglong dataset.
rows.evidence: Material 3-class classification (diffuse, specular, scattering) accuracy > 85% and AUC > 0.90 in Phase 1 validation.

rows.design_decision: Dual-mask physical modeling utilizing a medium mask for pixel-level roughness and an angular direction mask for the deflection vector field
rows.motivation: A single mask cannot simultaneously handle spatial occlusion (geometric issue) and angular non-Lambertian reflection (physical photometric inconsistency), introducing severe noise during 3D cost aggregation.
rows.sota_gap: Traditional light field matching costs (e.g., EPI variance) degrade to texture copying in non-Lambertian regions; dual masks explicitly filter occlusion and highlight noise during cost volume construction.
rows.scenario: Urban mixed scenes containing glass curtain wall reflections and complex spatial occlusions in the UrbanLF-Syn dataset.
rows.evidence: Mask-guided cost aggregation reduces the val_MAE of the Non-Lambertian subset from a baseline of 0.411 to < 0.25.

rows.design_decision: Component-aware depth estimation strategy applying EPI for diffuse, photometric stereo for specular, and model correction for scattering, fused via confidence weighting
rows.motivation: Different reflection types have completely different physical imaging mechanisms; using a unified depth regression network causes complete failure and depth jumps in specular regions due to violated view-consistency.
rows.sota_gap: Compared to end-to-end black-box depth estimation networks (e.g., LFNet, EPINET) that suffer from severe blurring in specular areas, component-aware strategy ensures depth continuity at edges and reflections via physical weight fusion.
rows.scenario: Scenes containing mixed reflection materials like metal and plastic in HCInew and Wanner_HCI datasets.
rows.evidence: Overall val_MAE < 0.20, Lambertian subset val_MAE < 0.16, Non-Lambertian subset val_MAE < 0.25.

rows.design_decision: Three-stage progressive validation framework with Phase Gates code-level constraints blocking subsequent training if physical hypotheses fail
rows.motivation: Deep learning models easily fall into local optima or overfit on small-sample non-Lambertian data; skipping physical validation for end-to-end training causes the model to lose physical interpretability.
rows.sota_gap: Traditional research directly optimizes a single end-to-end loss function; this framework blocks subsequent training via regex matching if physical hypotheses fail, avoiding 249 previous invalid training loops.
rows.scenario: Model training and hyperparameter tuning stages across all datasets, especially the small-sample Non-lambertian_zhenglong dataset.
rows.evidence: BRDF parameter constrained least squares estimation in Phase 2 achieves material weight error < 10%, ensuring physical consistency for Phase 3 fusion.

rows.design_decision: Physical Reflection Model (PRM) module based on the microfacet model to explicitly decompose pixel intensity into diffuse and specular components
rows.motivation: Pure data-driven feature extractors (LFFE) cannot understand the physical photometric consistency of non-Lambertian illumination, leading to artifacts during multi-view feature matching.
rows.sota_gap: Compared to methods using only 2D/3D CNNs to extract appearance features, PRM provides physically meaningful prior feature maps that separate view-dependent specularities from view-independent diffuse albedo.
rows.scenario: Highlight regions with strong view-dependence, such as glossy surfaces and metallic objects in the Non-lambertian_zhenglong dataset.
rows.evidence: Integrating PRM features significantly improves the signal-to-noise ratio of the 3D cost volume, supporting the overall val_MAE target of < 0.20.

rows.design_decision: Domain-balanced inverse frequency weighted sampling via WeightedRandomSampler and 2-98 percentile truncation normalization for GT disparity
rows.motivation: The distribution of Lambertian and Non-Lambertian scenes is highly imbalanced, and raw disparities contain extreme outliers; direct training causes the model to bias towards the majority class and overfit to outliers.
rows.sota_gap: Compared to standard uniform sampling and MSE loss, inverse frequency weighted sampling and percentile truncation effectively mitigate the long-tail distribution problem, improving generalization on minority non-Lambertian scenes.
rows.scenario: Mixed dataset containing 209 training scenes across HCInew, Wanner_HCI, Non-lambertian_zhenglong, and UrbanLF-Syn.
rows.evidence: Smoother loss convergence curves during training, with Non-Lambertian subset val_MAE stably maintained at < 0.25 without severe overfitting.
